zh.lifeSummary: 當Count Adolf IV. von Schauenburg und Holstein於1205年之前出生時，他的父親Count Adolf III of Schauenburg and Holstein44歲，母親Adelheid von Querfurt35歲。他於1223年與Heilwig zur Lippe在德國史萊斯維-爾斯坦可萊斯 斯坦波格易結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1261年7月8日在德國史萊斯維-爾斯坦吉爾，埋葬在德國史萊斯維-爾斯坦吉爾吉爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Count Adolf IV. von Schauenburg und Holstein于1205年之前出生时，他的父亲Count Adolf III of Schauenburg and Holstein44岁，母亲Adelheid von Querfurt35岁。他于1223年与Heilwig zur Lippe在德国史萊斯維-爾斯坦可萊斯 斯坦波格易结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1261年7月8日在德国史萊斯維-爾斯坦吉爾，埋葬在德国史萊斯維-爾斯坦吉爾吉爾。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: und
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Klaus, Otto, Wilhelm.

English: from the Middle English personal name Mund(e), in many cases representing Old English Munda, a pet form of names like Ēadmund (Middle English Edmund, common in East Anglia). In northern and eastern England the equivalent Old Norse Mundi (a pet form of names like Agmundr) would also have given rise to Middle English Mund(e).
German: variant of   Mundt  .

variants.name: Monday
meanings: English:

from the Middle English personal name Mundi, Mundy, usually a pet form of male personal names ending in -mund or -mond (see Mund , and compare Edmond , Hammond , and Simmonds ). Alternatively, and especially in northern and eastern England, the name might derive from Old Norse Mundi, a pet form of names like Old Norse Agmundr, Middle English Agemund.
nickname from Middle English Mone(n)day ‘Monday’. It is possibly a name given to one born on that day or to a holder of Mondayland, land held on condition of working for the lord on Mondays.

variants.name: Mundt
meanings: German:
nickname for someone with a prominent or striking mouth or whose mouth was in some other way remarkable, from Middle High German munt ‘mouth’.
nickname for a guardian or trustworthy person, from Middle High German munt ‘guardian, protector’.

variants.name: Munson
meanings: English (mainly Essex): patronymic from the personal name   Mund  , meaning ‘son of Mund’. See also   Munn  .
Americanized form of Norwegian and Danish   Monsen  . Compare   Munsen  .
Altered form of   Monson   1, a surname of Swedish origin.

nameMeanings.name: Holstein
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: habitational name from the province of Holstein, long disputed between Germany and Denmark. This gets its name from holsten, the dative plural, originally used after a preposition, of holst, from Middle Low German holt-sate ‘dweller in the woods’ (from Middle Low German holt ‘wood’ + sate, sete ‘tenant’). The final syllable has been erroneously altered by analogy with North German Steen ‘stone’, which in South German and standard German has the form Stein.

variants.name: Holstine
meanings: Americanized form of German   Holstein  .

variants.name: Hulstein
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Albertus, Kees.
Dutch vowel variant of German   Holstein  .

variants.name: Hollstein
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Hans, Heinz, Klaus, Kurt, Otto, Ulrich.
German: variant of   Holstein  , or perhaps a habitational name from Hollstein on the Werra River.

variants.name: Holst
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Jorgen, Erik, Vibeke, Anders, Carsten, Ludvig, Sven.
North German and Scandinavian: habitational name for someone from Holstein (see Holstein ), a derivative of German Holtsate, Danish Holtsete ‘person from Holstein’.
Dutch (also Van Holst): habitational name for someone from any of several places so called, from holst, hulst ‘holly (forest)’. Compare Hulst .

variants.name: Holsten
meanings: North German: variant of   Holstein   or   Holst  .

nameMeanings.name: Adolf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from Adelwolf, a compound of adal ‘noble’ + wolf ‘wolf’.

nameMeanings.name: IV.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Vivian   and   Vivien .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Count Adolf IV. von Schauenburg und Holstein was born before 1205, his father, Count Adolf III of Schauenburg and Holstein, was 44 and his mother, Adelheid von Querfurt, was 35. He married Heilwig zur Lippe in 1223, in Itzehoe, Steinburg, Schleswig-Holstein, Germany. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 8 July 1261, in Kiel, Schleswig-Holstein, Germany, and was buried in Kiel, Kiel, Schleswig-Holstein, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Count Adolf IV. von Schauenburg und Holstein est né avant 1205, son père, Count Adolf III of Schauenburg and Holstein, avait 44 ans et sa mère, Adelheid von Querfurt, avait 35 ans. Il a épousé Heilwig zur Lippe en 1223, à Itzehoe, Kreis Steinburg, Schleswig-Holstein, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 8 juillet 1261, à Kiel, Schleswig-Holstein, Allemagne, et a été enterré à Kiel, Kiel, Schleswig-Holstein, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Count Adolf IV. von Schauenburg und Holstein vor 1205 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Count Adolf III of Schauenburg and Holstein 44 und seine Mutter Adelheid von Querfurt 35. Er hat 1223 in Itzehoe, Kreis Steinburg, Schleswig-Holstein, Deutschland Heilwig zur Lippe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 8. Juli 1261 in Kiel, Schleswig-Holstein, Deutschland und wurde in Kiel, Kiel, Schleswig-Holstein, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Count Adolf IV. von Schauenburg und Holstein è nato il prima del 1205, il suo padre, Count Adolf III of Schauenburg and Holstein, aveva 44 anni e il suo madre, Adelheid von Querfurt, aveva 35 anni. Lui sposato Heilwig zur Lippe nel 1223, nel Itzehoe, Kreis Steinburg, Schleswig-Holstein, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 8 luglio 1261, nel Kiel, Schleswig-Holstein, Germania, e fu sepolto a Kiel, Kiel, Schleswig-Holstein, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Count Adolf IV. von Schauenburg und Holsteinが1205年以前に生まれた時，父Count Adolf III of Schauenburg and Holsteinは44歳，母Adelheid von Querfurtは35歳であった。1223年にドイツスチレスウィグ-hオルステインクレイス ステインブルグイツェーhオでHeilwig zur Lippeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1261年7月8日にドイツスチレスウィグ-hオルステインキエルで死去し，ドイツスチレスウィグ-hオルステインキエルキエルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Count Adolf IV. von Schauenburg und Holstein(이)가 1205년 이전에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Count Adolf III of Schauenburg and Holstein(은)는 44세였고, 그의 어머니 Adelheid von Querfurt(은)는 35세였다. 그는 1223년 독일 슐레스위그-홀스타인 크레이스 스타인버그 잇츠호에서 Heilwig zur Lippe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1261년 7월 8일 독일 슐레스위그-홀스타인 킬에서 사망했으며 독일 슐레스위그-홀스타인 킬 킬에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Count Adolf IV. von Schauenburg und Holstein nasceu antes 1205, seu pai, Count Adolf III of Schauenburg and Holstein, tinha 44 anos e sua mãe, Adelheid von Querfurt, tinha 35 anos. Ele casou-se com Heilwig zur Lippe em 1223, em Itzehoe, Kreis Steinburg, Schleswig-Holstein, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 8 de julho de 1261, em Kiel, Schleswig-Holstein, Alemanha, e foi sepultado em Kiel, Kiel, Schleswig-Holstein, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Count Adolf IV. von Schauenburg und Holstein родился перед 1205 г., его отцу по имени Count Adolf III of Schauenburg and Holstein было 44 года, а матери, которую звали Adelheid von Querfurt, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Heilwig zur Lippe в 1223 г. в местности ицэо, крис стинбург, щлесвиг-холстин, Германия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 8 июля 1261 г. в местности киел, щлесвиг-холстин, Германия и был похоронен в местности киел, киел, щлесвиг-холстин, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Count Adolf IV. von Schauenburg und Holstein nació antes de 1205, su papá, Count Adolf III of Schauenburg and Holstein, tenía 44 años y su mamá, Adelheid von Querfurt, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Heilwig zur Lippe en 1223, en Itzehoe, Kreis Steinburg, Schleswig-Holstein, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 8 de julio de 1261, en Kiel, Schleswig-Holstein, Alemania, y fue sepultado en Kiel, Kiel, Schleswig-Holstein, Alemania.
